Alabama Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
No Interstate Compact

How to Transfer Your Dietitian License to Alabama

There is no active interstate compact covering dietitian practice in Alabama. To work in Alabama, apply for licensure by endorsement through Alabama Board of Examiners for Dietetics/Nutrition Practice.

Endorsement fee
$250
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Available
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of supervised practice.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) national examination.

  3. Submit the Application

    Complete the licensure application and submit it to the Board with the $100 application fee and $150 initial license fee ($250 total).

  4. Provide Verification

    Submit a copy of your current, active CDR registration card or verification letter directly from CDR.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$250
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Renewal fee
$200
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years + 30 CE hours

Required Documents

  • CDR Registration Verification: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
  • Driver's License or Government ID: Verification of identity and legal presence in the U.S.
  • Official Transcripts: Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.

Board Contact

Board
Alabama Board of Examiners for Dietetics/Nutrition Practice
Department
Independent State Board
Phone
(334) 242-4505
Email

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $200. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years + 30 CE hours.

Alabama Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does an Alabama dietitian license cost?

The initial cost is $250, which includes a non-refundable $100 application fee and a $150 license fee. The temporary license costs $175.

When does the Alabama license need to be renewed?

The license is renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $200.

What are the continuing education requirements?

Alabama requires 30 hours of continuing professional education (CPE) during each two-year renewal cycle.

Does Alabama have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Alabama. To work in Alabama, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Alabama?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Alabama while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
